name: Onion Soup with Gorgonzola Toast
recipeInstructions:
1. Onion soup is a classic example of caramelization as a flavor enhancer. This soup gets an added flavor boost from the garnish—a slice of Gorgonzola topped with toast.
2. Cook onions and garlic in a large, heavy, covered soup pot over moderate heat until they release their moisture, about 10 minutes.
3. Add butter and cook uncovered until onions are a rich golden brown, about 15 minutes.
4. Add flour and thyme; cook for an additional 5 minutes, stirring constantly.
5. Add stock and tomato paste. Simmer for 15 minutes. Season with salt and pepper.
6. Place a 2-ounce slice of Gorgonzola on each piece of toast. Broil under the oven broiler until the cheese is bubbly, about 1 minute.
7. Ladle soup into warmed bowls and top with a gorgonzola toast.
PER SERVING:
370 calories, 18 grams protein, 41 grams carbohydrate, 15 grams fat (8 grams saturated), 36 milligrams cholesterol, 501 milligrams sodium, 4 grams fiber.
